title: Keeping historical packages in the new Ubuntu repositories
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**
Currently, on openSuseBuild server repos, only the packages from the latest release are installed.  There is no way to go back manually or via packaging tools.  Older packages are gone from the repo forever.   I believe that older podman and build dependencies should be available for downgrading and troubleshooting at least up to the last .0 release.  I.e. if we have 1.7.0, we should be able to go to 1.6.1 if needed.  Or at least the most stable version of 1.6.x should be preserved.   PPA kept other built packages available for download without a limit. 

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Check the repo: http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_18.04/
 and you will see that podman 1.6.4 is gone and replaced with podman 1.7.0


**Describe the results you received:**

Podman 1.6.4 is no longer avaialble

**Describe the results you expected:**

Older podman versions up to the point should be available together with all the build dependencies.

comments.body: Is there an update on this issue?  Ubuntu 1.8.0 released under testing but 1.7.0 have disappeared.  What prevents one from keeping both versions?
@alitvak69 hi, i had set automation up to delete older builds when newer builds show up, I'll check if OBS does keep older debs if I leave the older packaging sources in there..

so OBS people told me the repos won't keep the prior built debs after a new version is shipped. If it helps, I can keep the packaging sources (dsc + tarball) in there so you can fetch the source and build it if need be.

I think keeping sources will solve the problem for me.  It is easy enough
to build deb based on tarball and dsc.

On Mon, Feb 3, 2020, 2:48 PM Lokesh Mandvekar <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> so OBS people told me the repos won't keep the prior built debs after a
> new version is shipped. If it helps, I can keep the packaging sources (dsc
> + tarball) in there so you can fetch the source and build it if need be.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4937?email_source=notifications&email_token=ABWJN3H5CZB6SJT3NLFPXP3RBB7KXA5CNFSM4KKPWVEKYY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGOEKVK55A#issuecomment-581611252>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ABWJN3AZTGORTPGQB5QW373RBB7KXANCNFSM4KKPWVEA>
> .
>
@lsm5 Podman 1.8 had been removed from testing repo.  Neither source not .deb are available.
It looks like 1.7 dissapeared from stable repo on OBS yet again.  No source files either.  Is there at least a way keep sources and dsc in archive subfolder or something?
@lsm5 This is a duplicate of another issue, could you close one of them?

> It looks like 1.7 dissapeared from stable repo on OBS yet again. No source files either. Is there at least a way keep sources and dsc in archive subfolder or something?

older files have `.old` appended. See: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman . OBS wouldn't let me put stuff in subdirs, so that's why the name change.

Let's talk further in https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3924 if anything.
